questions.question: The Royal Bengal Tiger is a famous animal of ________.
questions.explanation: Royal Bengal Tiger, Turtles, crocodiles, gharials, and snakes are found in Mangrove forests. Among them, Royal Bengal Tiger is a famous animal in these forests.

questions.question: Peepal trees belong to which one of the following types of forest?
questions.explanation: In Tropical dry deciduous forests there are open stretches in which Peepal grow.

questions.question: Mangrove forests can thrive in
questions.explanation: Mangrove Forests can survive in saline water. They are found mainly in Sunderbans in West Bengal and in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

questions.question: Mahogany and rosewood trees are found in.
questions.explanation: Tropical Evergreen Forests are also called tropical rain forests. They occur in the areas which receive heavy rainfall. Many species of trees are found in these forests, which shed their leaves at different times of the year. Therefore, they always appear green and are called evergreen forest. Important trees are Mahogany, Ebony, and Rosewood.

questions.question: Chir, pine, deodar are important trees of which forest:
questions.explanation: At a height between 1500 metres and 2500 metres most of the trees are conical in shape. These trees are called coniferous trees. Chir, Pine, Deodar are Important trees in Mountain Vegetation.

questions.question: Sunderban in:
questions.explanation: Mangrove plants can survive in saline water. They are mainly found in the Sunderbans in West Bengal and in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. Sundari is a well known tree in the mangrove forest of Sunderban.

questions.question: Sundari is well known species of:
questions.explanation: Mangrove plants can survive in saline water. They are mainly found in the Sunderbans in West Bengal and in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. Sundari is a well known tree in the mangrove forest of Sunderban.

questions.question: Tropical deciduous forest are also called
questions.explanation: Tropical deciduous forest is also called monsoon forests. The trees of deciduous forest shed their leaves at a particular time of the year. Hence, such a forest is called a deciduous forest.

questions.question: In which type of vegetation are the cactus, Khair, Babool, etc found?
questions.explanation: Cactus, Khair, babool, keekar are found in the states of Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana, Eastern slopes of Western Ghats, and Gujarat. Cactus, Khair, Babool, etc found in desert vegetation.

questions.question: Forest always appear green are called
questions.explanation: Tropical rainforest is found in those areas which receive heavy rainfall. The tropical rainforest is so dense that sunlight does not reach the ground. The trees of the tropical rainforest shed their leaves at different times of the year. Due to this, the forest always appears green. Hence, such a forest is called evergreen forest.

questions.question: Mangrove forest are found in
questions.explanation: Mangrove Forests can survive in saline water. They are found mainly in Sunderbans in West Bengal and in the Andaman and  Nicobar Islands.

questions.question: In Mangrove forest Sundari is a well known
questions.explanation: Mangrove plants can survive in saline water. They are mainly found in the Sunderbans in West Bengal and in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. Sundari is a well-known tree in the mangrove forest of Sunderban.

questions.question: The leaves of which tree are in the form of spines to reduce the loss of water.
questions.explanation: Thorny Bushes type of vegetation is found in dry areas. The leaves are in the form of spines. This helps the plant to reduce the loss of water. The leaves of  Cactus, Khair, babool, and keekar are in the form of spines to reduce the loss of water.

questions.question: Where is the India most forest located?
questions.explanation: India forest located where heavy rainfall occur. Andaman and  Nicobar Islands, parts of north-eastern states and a narrow strip of the western slope of the western ghats are found more forest.

questions.question: In which part of India are evergreen forest located?
questions.explanation: Evergreen forests are found in the areas receiving heavy rainfall and are so dense that sunlight does not reach the ground. In India are evergreen forest located at Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Tripura, West Bengal, and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

questions.question: Reserved forests where natural vegetation beauty and wildlife are preserved in their natural environment:
questions.explanation: In order to protect wildlife many national parks, sanctuaries, and biosphere reserves have been set up. National park is a reserved forest where natural vegetation beauty and wildlife are preserved in their natural environment. Jim Corbett National Park is the oldest national park in India and was established in 1936 as Hailey National Park to protect the endangered Bengal tiger.

questions.question: Which of the following trees are not found in tropical deciduous forest?
questions.explanation: Rosewood found in tropical evergreen forest. Sal, teak, peepal, neem, and sheesham are found in tropical deciduous forest.

questions.question: Where are the mangrove forest found?
questions.explanation: Mangrove forests are found in Ganga-Brahmaputra delta, Krishna, Kaveri, Godavari, and Mahanadi delta.

questions.question: Which one of the following states does not have the Tropical deciduous forests?
questions.explanation: MP, UP, Bihar, Jharkhand, Chattisgarh, Odisha, and in parts of Maharashtra have the Tropical deciduous forests but Rajasthan has horny Bushes forest.

questions.question: Animals, birds, and fish leading a free life in their natural surroundings:
questions.explanation: Animals, birds, and fish that live in natural vegetation are called wild animals. Thousands of animal species live in forests.

questions.question: Which of the following is not the feature of mountain vegetation?

options.option: Sal, teak, peepal, neem, and shisham are important trees of these forests.
Chir, Pine and Deodar are important trees of these forests.
A wide range of species is found in the mountains according to the variation in height.

questions.explanation: In Mountain Vegetation a wide range of species is found in the mountains according to the variation in height.  At a height between 1500 metres and 2500 metres most of the trees are conical in shape. These trees are called coniferous trees. Important trees are Chir, Pine, Deodar. sal, teak, peepal, neem, and shisham are important trees int tropical Deciduous Forests.

questions.question: It is a policy of protecting and preserving the forest cover and wildlife:
questions.explanation: Forest conservation is the practice of planting and maintaining forested areas for the benefit and sustainability of future generations. The conservation of forest also stands & aims at a quick shift in the composition of trees species and age distribution. Forest conservation is a policy of protecting and preserving the forest cover and wildlife.

questions.question: Vegetation found in Thar includes thorn, scrub vegetation of the xerophytic type:
questions.explanation: Desert Vegetation found in Thar, includes thorn, scrub vegetation of the xerophytic type.

questions.question: Birds those migrate to our country in winter season every year from Siberia are called
questions.explanation: Birds those migrate to our country in winter season every year from Siberia are called Migratory birds. Some birds migrate to our country in the winter season every year such as Pelican, Siberian Crane, Stork, Flamingo, Pintail Duck, Siberian migrate from Siberia. They arrive in December and stay till early March.

questions.question: The most common tree in the monsoon forest is:
questions.explanation: Tropical Deciduous Forests are also called monsoon forests. Sal, teak, peepal, neem, and shisham are important trees in monsoon forest.

questions.question: In which of the following areas tropical deciduous forests are not found?
questions.explanation: Tropical Deciduous Forest in India is Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Orissa, and parts of Maharashtra.

questions.question: ______ trees are called coniferous trees.
questions.explanation: In Mountain Vegetation a wide range of species is found in the mountains according to the variation in height. At a height between 1500 metres and 2500 metres most of the trees are conical in shape. These trees are called coniferous trees.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a national park?
questions.explanation: The Gir Forest National Park and Wildlife Sanctuary is a forest and wildlife sanctuary near Talala Gir in Gujarat, India. Periyar National Park and Wildlife Sanctuary (PNP) is a protected area near Thekkady in the districts of Idukki, Kottayam, and Pathanamthitta in Kerala, India. Idukki National Park is a beautiful wildlife sanctuary, full of natural splendours and wildlife wonders. Bharatpur is not a national park.

questions.question: In Assam there is national park for
questions.explanation: Kaziranga National Park (pronounced Assamese pronunciation: [kaziɹɔŋa ɹastɹiɔ uɪddan]) is a national park in the Golaghat and Nagaon districts of the state of Assam, India. The sanctuary, which hosts two-thirds of the world's great one-horned rhinoceroses, is a World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Which of the following tree are conical in shape?
questions.explanation: At a height between 1500 metres and 2500 metres most of the trees are conical in shape. These trees are called coniferous trees. Chir, Pine, Deodar are conical in shape.

questions.question: Which of the following trees is found in the tropical deciduous forest?
questions.explanation: Sal, teak, peepal, neem, and shisham trees are found in the tropical deciduous forest.

questions.question: Bulk of timber trees found in:
questions.explanation: Chir, Pine, Deodar are called coniferous trees. and sal, teak, peepal, neem and shisham are Tropical Deciduous Forests trees. So, Bulk of timber trees found in Deciduous and Coniferous forest.

questions.question: Sal, teak, peepal, neem, and shisham are example of trees found in:
questions.explanation: Sal, teak, peepal, neem, and shisham are an important tree in Tropical Deciduous Forests.

questions.question: Altitudes is an important factor in the distribution of vegetation in the
questions.explanation: Different types of species are found in the mountains at different altitudes. At the height between 1500 meter and 2500 meter, most of the trees are conical in shape. Due to this, they are called coniferous trees.

questions.question: Which of the following is not the feature of deciduous forest?

options.option: They are found in the areas of 100-200 cm rainfall
They shed their leaves in the particular season

questions.explanation: Tropical deciduous forest is also called monsoon forests. The trees of deciduous forest shed their leaves at a particular time of the year. Hence, such a forest is called a deciduous forest. They are tall is not the feature of deciduous forest.

questions.question: Where does dry thorn forest occur?

options.option: Where rain fall less than 505 cm occur
Where rain fall less than 250 cm occur
Where rain fall less than 150 cm occur

questions.explanation: Dry thorn forests are found in dry regions means where rainfall less than 50 cm occur.

questions.question: Height of trees found in Mountain vegetation is:
questions.explanation: In Mountain Vegetation a wide range of species is found in the mountains according to the variation in height. Height of trees found in Mountain vegetation is between 1500 metres and 2500 metres and most of the trees are conical in shape.

questions.question: Where are mangrove forests found?

options.option: The areas of coast influenced by tides

questions.explanation: The Mangrove forest are found in the river deltas along the sea coasts in India. The mangrove tidal forests are found in the areas of coasts influenced by tides. Mud and silt get accumulated on such coasts.

questions.question: In which of the following state's forests you will find one-horned rhinoceros?
questions.explanation: The one horned rhinoceros are found in the jungles of Assam and West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following region of India possesses tropical evergreen forests?
questions.explanation: Tropical Evergreen Forests are restricted to heavy rainfall areas of the Western Ghats and the island groups of Lakshadweep, Andaman and Nicobar, upper parts of Assam and Tamil Nadu coast.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a tree of tropical evergreen forests?
questions.explanation: Some of the commercially important trees of tropical evergreen forests are ebony, mahogany, rosewood, rubber, and cinchona. Acacias are found in The Thorny Forests.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most widespread vegetation of India?
questions.explanation: Tropical Deciduous Forests are the most widespread forests of India. They are also called the monsoon forests and spread over the region receiving rainfall between 200 cm and 70 cm.

questions.question: A region receives annual rainfall between 200 cm to 70 cm. Which of the following is the expected type of vegetation in the region?
questions.explanation: Tropical Deciduous Forests are also called the monsoon forests and spread over the region receiving rainfall between 200 cm and 70 cm.

questions.question: Under which of the following type of vegetation trees shed their leaves for about 6 to 8 weeks in dry summer?
questions.explanation: Trees of the most widespread Tropical Deciduous forests shed their leaves for about six to eight weeks in dry summer.

questions.question: Which of the following type of vegetation is found in areas receiving rainfall between 200 cm and 100 cm?
questions.explanation: On the basis of the availability of water, these forests are further divided into moist and dry deciduous. The moist deciduous forests are found in areas receiving rainfall between 200 and 100 cm.

questions.question: Which of the following type of vegetation is found in areas having rainfall between 100 cm and 70 cm?
questions.explanation: On the basis of the availability of water, these forests are further divided into moist and dry deciduous. The dry deciduous forests are found in areas having rainfall between 100 cm and 70 cm.

questions.question: Which is the most important tree of moist deciduous forests?
questions.explanation: Teak, Bamboos, sal, shisham, sandalwood, khair, kusum, arjun, mulberry are other commercially important species of Moist deciduous forests. Among them, Teak is the most dominant species of this forest.

questions.question: Which of the following type of vegetation is found in regions with less than 70 cm of rainfall?
questions.explanation: In regions with less than 70 cm of rainfall, the natural vegetation consists of thorny trees and bushes.

questions.question: Which of the following regions of India has thorn forests?
questions.explanation: Thorn type of vegetation is found in the north-western part of the country including semi-arid areas of Gujarat, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Uttar Pradesh, and Haryana.

questions.question: Why leaves of thorn forests mostly thick and small?

options.option: So that plants can get minimum sunlight.

questions.explanation: In Thorn forests, trees are scattered and have long roots penetrating deep into the soil in order to get moisture. The stems are succulent to conserve water. Leaves have a special feature. To minimize evaporation, leaves are mostly thick and small.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a feature of the thorn forest?

options.option: Trees are scattered and have long roots.

questions.explanation:
Thorn Forest has the following characteristics:

  1. Acacias, palms, euphorbias, and cacti are the main plant species.
  2. Trees are scattered and have long roots penetrating deep into the soil in order to get moisture. The stems are succulent to conserve water.
  3. Leaves are mostly thick and small to minimize evaporation.

However, trees reach great heights up to 60m in tropical evergreen forests.

questions.question: Which of the following is a deciding factor for the type of vegetation found in the Himalayas?

options.option: The decrease in rainfall with increasing altitude.
The increasing in temperature with decreasing altitude.
The decrease in temperature with increasing altitude.
The increasing in rainfall with increasing altitude.

questions.explanation: The character and extent of vegetation are mainly determined by temperature along with humidity in the air, precipitation, and soil. On the slopes of the Himalayas and the hills of the Peninsula above the height of 915 metres, the fall in the temperature affects the types of vegetation and its growth and changes it from tropical to subtropical.

questions.question: Which of the following tree predominates in the Himalayas between 1500m and 3000m?
questions.explanation: Between 1500 and 3000 metres, temperate forests containing coniferous trees like pine, deodar, silver fir, spruce, and cedar, are found.

questions.question: Which of the following animal is not found in montane forests?
questions.explanation: The common animals found in Montane forests are Kashmir stag, spotted dear, wild sheep, jackrabbit, Tibetan antelope, yak, snow leopard, squirrels, shaggy horn wild ibex, bear and rare red panda, sheep, and goats with thick hair. One-horned rhinoceros are found in the jungles of Assam and West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following type of vegetation is found in the areas of coasts influenced by tides?
questions.explanation: Dense mangroves are the common varieties of tidal forests found in the areas of coasts influenced by tides with roots of the plants submerged underwater.

questions.question: Which is the most important tree of the mangrove forests?
questions.explanation: Mangrove forests are found in Ganga Bhramputra delta where the most important Sundari trees are found, which provide durable hard timber.

questions.question: Which is the famous animal of the mangrove forests?
questions.explanation: Royal Bengal Tiger, Turtles, Crocodiles, Gharials, and Snakes are found in mangrove forests. Among them, the Royal Bengal Tiger is very famous.

questions.question: In which of the following states elephants are found?
questions.explanation: The elephants are the most majestic animals among the mammals. They are found in the hot wet forests of Assam, Karnataka and Kerala.

questions.question: In which of the following region/state the wild ass is found?
questions.explanation: Arid areas of the Rann of Kachchh are the habitat for wild ass.

questions.question: Which of the following is the natural habitat of the Indian lion?
questions.explanation: The natural habitat of the Indian lion is the Gir forest in Gujarat. Also, the Gir Forest is the last remaining habitat of the Asiatic lion.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a natural habitat of tiger?
questions.explanation: Tigers are found in the forests of Madhya Pradesh, the Sundarbans of West Bengal, and the Himalayan region. Hence, forests of Assam are not a natural habitat of the tiger.

questions.question: Which of the following has not been included in the world network of Biosphere Reserves?
questions.explanation: Fourteen biosphere reserves have been set up in the country to protect flora and fauna. Four out of these, the Sunderbans in the West Bengal, Nanda Devi in Uttaranchal, the Gulf of Mannar in Tamil Nadu, and the Nilgiris (Kerala, Karnataka and Tamil Nadu) have been included in the world network of Biosphere reserves. Manas is not included in Biosphere Reserve.

questions.question: Which of the following is not correctly matched?
questions.explanation: The Dudhwa National Park is a national park in the Terai of Uttar Pradesh, India.

questions.question: Cinchona trees are found in the areas of rainfall more than ________.
questions.explanation: Cinchona trees are found in Tropical Rain Forests. Such forests have rainfall more than 100 cms.

questions.question: In which of the following states is the Simlipal bio reserve located?
questions.explanation: Simlipal National Park is a national park and a tiger reserve in the Mayurbhanj district in the Indian state of Orissa.

questions.question: Which one of the following bio reserve of India is not included in the world network of bio reserve?
questions.explanation: The Sunderbans in the West Bengal, Nanda Devi in Uttaranchal, the Gulf of Mannar in Tamil Nadu, Manas, and the Nilgiris (Kerala, Karnataka, and TamilNadu) have been included in the world network of Biosphere reserves.

questions.question: Khana bird sanctuary is situated at:
questions.explanation: This sanctuary is located in Bharatpur (Rajasthan)and is famous for fauna sanctuary that hosts thousands of birds, especially during the winter season. The park is home to over 370 species of birds and animals. It was declared a protected sanctuary in 1971. It is also a World Heritage Site.

questions.question: What leads to change in the vegetation of the mountainous areas?

options.option: The decrease in temperature with increasing altitude
Its geographical location closer to the north pole

questions.explanation: The decrease in temperature with increasing altitude

questions.question: At what height the wet temperate type of forests is found?
questions.explanation: 1000 and 2000 meters

questions.question: Where do we find temperate grasslands?
questions.explanation: At the higher elevations

questions.question: Which vegetation is found at a height of more than 3,600 meters above sea level?
questions.explanation: Alpine

questions.question: Which of the following forests are found in coastal areas?
questions.explanation: Mangrove tidal forests

questions.question: Where in India are the sundari trees found?
questions.explanation: Ganga Brahmaputra delta

questions.question: What do sundari trees provide?
questions.explanation: Durable hard timber

questions.question: Which of the following animal is not found in this forests?
questions.explanation: Elephants are found in the hot wet forests of Assam, Karnataka and Kerala

questions.question: Migrations change the number, distribution and composition of the population in:

options.option: both the area of departure and arrival

questions.explanation: Migration is an important determinant of population change. It changes not only the population size but also the population composition of urban and rural populations both terms of area of departure and arrival.

questions.question: A large proportion of children in a population is a result of:
questions.explanation: Birthrate is a major component of growth because in India, birth rates have always been higher than death rates.High Birth Rate is responsible for a large proportion of children in a population.

questions.question: The magnitude of population growth refers to:

options.option: the total population of an area
the number of females per thousand males
the rate at which the population increases
the number of persons added each year

questions.explanation: Growth of population refers to the change in the number of inhabitants of a country/territory during a specific period of time, say during the last ten years.The total population of an area is the magnitude of population growth.

questions.question: According to the Census 2001, a literate person is one who

options.option: is 7-year-old and can read and write any language with understanding

questions.explanation:
Literacy is a very important quality of a
population.According to the Census of 2001, a person aged 7 years and above who can read and write with understanding in any language, is treated as literate.

questions.question: Census provides us with the information regarding
questions.explanation: Census is an official count or survey, especially of a population. Thus it provides all the information regarding the population.

questions.question: Kerala has a sex ratio of
questions.explanation: Sex ratio is defined as the number of females per 1000 males in the population.As per population census, Kerala has the highest sex ratio. It has 1,058 females per 1,000 males.

questions.question: Activities such as transport, communication, commerce, administration, etc., are included in
questions.explanation: Tertiary activities consist of industries which provide a service. It includes transport, communications, commerce, administration and other services.

questions.question: Which major section of the population needs 'greater attention' according to the NPP 2000?
questions.explanation: NPP 2000 identified adolescents as one of the major sections of the population that need greater attention. Besides nutritional requirements, the policy put greater emphasis on other important needs of adolescents including protection from unwanted pregnancies and sexually transmitted diseases (STD).

questions.question:
Which one from the following refers to Census?

  1. The process of taking a count of the total number of people in a country.
  2. The process of counting the number of states in a country.
  3. The process of counting the land area in a country.
  4. The process of counting the mountains and hilly terrains in a country.
questions.explanation: Census is the process of taking count of the total population of the country. In India, census is held every ten years.

questions.question: Census is conducted after how many years in India?
questions.explanation: Census is the process of taking count of the total population of the country. In India, it is held for every ten years.

questions.question: What was India’s population as per the 2001 census?
questions.explanation: India’s population has been steadily increasing from 361 million in 1951 to 1028 million in 2001.

questions.question: Which of the following provides us information regarding the population of our country?
questions.explanation: The census of India is an official count or survey, especially of a population. Thus, it provides information about the population.

questions.question: What is India’s share in the world population?
questions.explanation: India's population is equivalent to 17.7% of the total world population.

questions.question: What is the total area of India?
questions.explanation: India is the seventh-largest country in the world, with a total area of 3.28 million square kilometres.

questions.question: Which is the most populous state of India?
questions.explanation: As of 2011, with an estimated population of 199,581,477. Uttar Pradesh is the most populous state in India. Uttar Pradesh occupies 6.88 percent of India's land surface area but is home to 16.49 percent of India's population.

questions.question: Which is the least populous state of India?
questions.explanation: Sikkim is India's least populous state, with 610,577(0.6 million) inhabitants according to the 2011 census. Sikkim is also one of the least densely populated Indian states, with only 86 persons per square kilometre.

questions.question:
Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, and Jammu & Kashmir have a very low density of population. Which of the following is/are the reason/reasons for the same?

  1. Hilly Dissected and rocky nature of the terrain.
  2. Moderate to low rainfall.
  3. Shallow and less fertile soil.
  4. Low literacy level.
questions.explanation: Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, Jammu Kashmir, Assam and most of the Peninsular states have moderate population densities. Hilly, dissected, and rocky nature of the terrain, moderate to low rainfall, shallow, and less fertile soils have influenced population densities in these areas.

questions.question: Which is the largest state of India in terms of area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest state in India (in terms of area). It covers an area of 342, 239 square kilometres. This represents 10.4% of the whole India.

subtopics.name: Population Growth and Process of Population Change

questions.question: How is the density of population calculated?
questions.explanation: To calculate the population density, the population is divided by the size of the area.

questions.question: What was the density of population of India in 2001?
questions.explanation: Density of the population is defined as the number of persons per sq.km. The population density of India in 2001 was 324 persons per sq. km.

questions.question: Which state has the highest density of population?
questions.explanation: West Bengal has the highest density which is 904 persons per sq km.

questions.question: Which state has the lowest density of population?
questions.explanation: Arunachal Pradesh has the lowest density of population with only 13person per sq km.

questions.question: Which country has the most population in the world?
questions.explanation: In terms of population size, China is the most populated country.

questions.question: Which of the following is not responsible for making the population a dynamic phenomenon?
questions.explanation: The population is a dynamic phenomenon. The numbers, distribution, and composition of the population are constantly changing. This is the influence of the interaction of the three processes, namely-births, deaths, and migrations. Hence, education is not responsible.

questions.question: Choose the right word: To change in the number of inhabitants of country/territory during a specific period of time.
questions.explanation:
Growth of population refers to the change in the number of inhabitants of a country/territory during a specific period of time, say during the last ten years. Such a change can be expressed in two ways:

  1. in terms of absolute numbers.
  2. in terms of percentage change per year.

questions.question:
How is growth of population expressed ?
A. It can be expressed in terms of absolute number.
B. It can be expressed in terms of percentage change per year.
questions.explanation: Growth of population refers to the change in the number of inhabitants of a country/territory during a specific period of time, say during the last ten years. Such a change can be expressed in two ways: in terms of absolute numbers and in terms of percentage change per year.

questions.question: The population of India was 846.4 million in 1991 whereas it rose to 1028.7 million in 2001. What is the absolute increase in population?
questions.explanation: The absolute numbers added each year or decade is the magnitude of increase. It is obtained by simply subtracting the earlier population (e.g. that of 1991) from the later population (e.g. that of 2001). It is referred to as the absolute increase.Hence absolute increase is 1028.7 million - 846.4 million = 182.32 million.

questions.question: What is the natural increase in population?

options.option: Number of deaths per thousand persons in a year.
Number of live births per thousand persons in a year.
Sum of birth rate and death rate.
Difference between birth rate and death rates.

questions.explanation: The natural increase of population is the difference between birth rates and death rates.

questions.question: What is the birth rate?

options.option: Difference between birth rate and death rates.
Number of births per ten thousand persons in a year.
Sum of birth rate and death rate.
Number of births per thousand persons in a year.

questions.explanation: The birth rate is the number of live births per thousand persons in a year. It is a major component of growth because, in India, birth rates have always been higher than death rates.

questions.question: ________ is the number of deaths per thousand persons in a year.
questions.explanation: Death rate is the number of deaths per thousand persons in a year. The main cause of the rate of growth of the Indian population has been the rapid decline in death rates.

questions.question: What is migration?

options.option: It is in the composition of population.
It is the movement of goods and services.
It is the movement of people across regions and territories.
It is the movement of people, goods and services.

questions.explanation: The third component of population growth is migration. Migration is the movement of people across regions and territories. Migration can be internal (within the country) or international (between the countries).

questions.question: How many million plus cities are their in India ?

In which of the following age group the dependency ratio will be the highest ?
A. Below 15 years
B. Between 15-59 years
questions.explanation:
Children (generally below 15 years) :They are economically unproductive and need to be provided with food, clothing, education and medical care.

Working Age (15-59 years) :They are economically productive and biologically reproductive. They comprise the working population.

The percentage of children and the aged affect the dependency ratio because these groups are not producers.

questions.question: What was sex ratio of India in 2001?
questions.explanation: The sex ratio is defined as the number of females per 1000 males in the population. As per population census carried out in India, the sex ratio in India was 933.

questions.question: What was the literacy rate in India as per the Census of 2001?
questions.explanation: There has been a steady improvement in the literacy levels in India. The literacy rate in the country as per the Census of 2001 was 64.84 %

questions.question: What was the literacy rate of the male in India as per the Census of 2001?
questions.explanation: There has been a steady improvement in the literacy levels in India. The literacy rate in the country as per the Census of 2001 is 64.84 percent, 75.26 percent for males.

questions.question: What was the literacy rate of females in India in 2001?
questions.explanation: There has been a steady improvement in the literacy levels in India. The literacy rate in the country as per the Census of 2001 is 64.84 percent, 53.67 percent for females.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a primary activity?
questions.explanation: Primary activities include agriculture, animal husbandry, forestry, fishing, mining, and quarrying, etc. Transport includes in Tertiary Sector.

questions.question: Which of the following is a secondary activity?
questions.explanation: Secondary activities include manufacturing industry, building, and construction work, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following is a tertiary activity?
questions.explanation: Tertiary activities include transport, banking, communications, commerce, administration, and other services.

questions.question: What was the life expectancy at birth in 2001?
questions.explanation: Life expectancy at birth has increased from 36.7 years in 1951 to 64.6 years in 2001. Approx 65 years.

questions.question: Which of the following programmes aims towards encouraging delayed marriage and child-bearing, education of adolescents?
questions.explanation: National Population Policy(NPP 2000) identified adolescents as one of the major sections of the population that need greater attention. It called for programmes that aim towards encouraging delayed marriage and child-bearing, education of adolescents about the risks of unprotected sex, making contraceptive services accessible and affordable, providing food supplements, nutritional services, strengthening legal measures to prevent child marriage.

questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for a large proportion of children in a population?
questions.explanation: The birth rate is a major component of growth because, in India, birth rates have always been higher than death rates. A large proportion of children in a population is a result of High Birth Rates.

questions.question: Which state in India has the lowest density of population?
questions.explanation: Arunachal Pradesh has the lowest density of population in India. According to the 2011 census of India, the total population of Arunachal Pradesh is roughly 1.4 million on an area of 84,000 km2, amounting to a population density of about 17 km−2 far below the Indian average of 370 km−2.

questions.question: What is the migration pattern in India?

options.option: There is not much migration in India

questions.explanation: From rural to urban

questions.question: What factor in rural area is responsible for the migration?
questions.explanation: Push factor

options.option: For the better civil services and education facilities
For the better environment and spacious places
For increased employment opportunities and better living conditions

questions.explanation: For increased employment opportunities and better living conditions

questions.question: What capital is needed to set up a jaggery manufacturing unit?

Tools, machines, buildings form fixed capital while raw materials (which is sugarcane) and money in hand are working capital.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not an effect of the modern farming?
questions.explanation:
Effects of modern farming techniques:

  • It typically requires the use of chemical fertilizers, resulting degradation of soil quality.
  • The rain dissolves about half of the nitrate in chemical fertilizers used on crops. This pollutes water courses as the dissolved nitrate runs off the lands.
  • In some places, water supplies are inadequate, and modern farming methods require excessive water usage, which will eventually result in water shortages and groundwater depletion.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to measure poverty?
questions.explanation: Poverty is measured on the basis of a person's income or consumption level. If it falls below a given minimum level which is necessary to fulfill basic needs, he is considered poor.

questions.explanation:
PAP was not among the three important food intervention programmers introduced by the government. The three important were:

  1. Public Distribution System (PDS): This system was made to ensure a smooth supply of subsidized food items to the poor.
  2. Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS): These services were introduced to provide proper nutrition to the poor children.
  3. Food For Work (FFW): This programme was introduced to help the poor earn food in return of some work and also to provide them with employment.
